steps.title: Understanding the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the y-axis. By definition, this occurs where x = 0.

steps.title: Reading the value from the graph
steps.content: Looking at the graph where it crosses the y-axis (at x = 0), the curve passes through the point (0, 8). This matches the correct answer — D is correct.
